zh.lifeSummary: 當Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo於1233年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多時，他的父親Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León32歲，母親Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen36歲。他於大約1256年與Laure Monfort D'epernon結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1261年10月27日在他的家鄉享年28歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯布爾戈斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo于1233年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多时，他的父亲Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León32岁，母亲Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen36岁。他于大约1256年与Laure Monfort D'epernon结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1261年10月27日在他的家乡享年28岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯布爾戈斯。

nameMeanings.name: Fernández,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Jorge, Miguel, Fernando, Mario, Jesus, Pedro, Francisco.  Portuguese Ligia, Joao, Armanda, Godofredo, Wenceslao.

Spanish (Fernández): patronymic from the personal name Fernando . The surname (and to a lesser extent the variant Hernandez ) has also been established in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some Jewish descendants retained the surname that their families had adopted as Roman Catholics.

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Orlando, Aida, Carlos, Jesusa, Rodolfo, Ruben, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.  Indian Rohan, Lalith, Nimal, Rajiv, Sanjeeva, Shanthi, Anil, Anusha, Dushan, Kapila, Lakshman, Mallika.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Fernando, a younger form of Ferdinando (see Ferdinand ). This surname is also found in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there, and as a result of the expulsion of the Jews from Spain and Portugal at the end of the 15th century, many of whom moved to Italy. It is also common in western India and especially in Sri Lanka, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sevilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jesus, Miguel, Rafael, Jorge, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Mario, Pedro, Ramon.

Spanish: habitational name from the city of Seville (Sevilla in Spanish), the capital of Andalusia, in southwestern Spain. The city is extremely ancient, having reputedly been founded by the Phoenicians. The origin of the name is obscure, presumably Phoenician. It is first recorded in the Latin form Hispalis, which was adopted in Arabic as Isbilia, and thence into early Spanish as Sibilia, now Sevilla.

variants.name: Sevillano
meanings: Spanish: habitational name for someone from Seville (see   Sevilla  ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toledo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Jorge, Manuel, Ramon, Rafael, Juan, Antonio, Jesus, Miguel, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco.

Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from the city in central Spain, which was the capital of the Visigothic state between the 6th and 8th centuries. Its role declined for three centuries after the Muslim invasion of Spain, until it was taken as the capital of the kingdom of Castile between the 11th and 16th centuries. It was a major cultural and political center throughout the Middle Ages, and was also the home of an important Jewish community. The placename, first recorded in Latin as Toletum, is of obscure etymology, possibly connected with Toleto in Piedmont; Jewish tradition connects it with Hebrew toledot ‘generations’, but this is no more than folk etymology. Compare Toledano .

Jewish (Sephardic): also an adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the surname their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Toledano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shlomo, Igal, Moshe, Shimon, Ehud, Shmuel, Yehuda.  Spanish Alberto, Gonzalo, Alina, Carlos, Conrado, Elena, Enrique, Evelia, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramona.  French Gabrielle, Michel.
Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Toledo (see   Toledo  ), from an adjectival form of the placename.

nameMeanings.name: Sancho
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin, perhaps from Latin sanctus ‘holy’. It will always be associated with Sancho Panza , Don Quixote's companion.

en.lifeSummary: When Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo was born in 1233, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, his father, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, was 32 and his mother, Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen, was 36. He married Laure Monfort D'epernon about 1256. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 27 October 1261, in his hometown, at the age of 28, and was buried in Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo est né en 1233, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Laure Monfort D'epernon environ 1256. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 27 octobre 1261, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterré à Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne.

ko.lifeSummary: Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo(이)가 1233년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen(은)는 36세였다. 그는 대략 1256년 Laure Monfort D'epernon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1261년 10월 27일 그의 고향에서 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 버고스 버고스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo nasceu em 1233, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Laure Monfort D'epernon aproximadamente 1256. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 27 de outubro de 1261, em sua cidade natal, com 28 anos, e foi sepultado em Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo родился в 1233 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, его отцу по имени Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Laure Monfort D'epernon около 1256 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 27 октября 1261 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 28 лет и был похоронен в местности бургос, бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sancho Fernández, Arzobispo de Sevilla y de Toledo nació en 1233, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, su papá, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Laure Monfort D'epernon aproximadamente 1256. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 27 de octubre de 1261, en su pueblo, a la edad de 28 años, y fue sepultado en Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, España.
